question: Which sequence correctly adds a variable to a newly generated scope during modification mode in Operate?

options.text: Right-click the new scope in the diagram, choose `Inject Variable`, and the variable is committed at once.
Select the new scope in the instance history, click `Add Variable` in the variables panel, fill in Name and Value, then blur out of the last edited field.
Apply the pending modifications first, then open the new scope and add the variable via the variables panel.
Set the variable on the parent process scope; child scopes inherit it automatically once modifications are applied.

a.text: The docs describe no right-click `Inject Variable` action, and changes are pending, not committed on the spot.

b.text: Selecting the new scope in the instance history, clicking `Add Variable`, entering Name and Value, then blurring out is the documented four-step sequence that adds the variable to pending modifications.

c.text: Applying ends modification mode; the docs explicitly add variables while still in modification mode.

d.text: Operate does not document automatic inheritance of variables from parent to child scopes for this feature.

explanation: Adding a variable to a new scope is a four-step flow in modification mode: select the new scope in the instance history, click `Add Variable` in the variables panel, fill Name and Value, blur out of the field. With valid values, the new variable lands in pending modifications.
